coles.name: Coles
coles.programmeName: Coles Together to Zero & Together to Zero Hunger sustainability programmes
coles.portalName: Coles Supplier Portal and Sedex / SAQ
coles.intro: Coles Group requires its food, grocery, packaging, logistics and services suppliers to provide structured sustainability data covering emissions, packaging recycled content, modern slavery and food waste. Coles publicly committed to net zero by 2050 with intermediate scope 3 targets — that flows through to every supplier.
whatTheyAsk: Scope 1, 2 and 3 emissions data covering products supplied to Coles
Packaging composition, recycled content and APCO targets compliance
Modern Slavery Act statement (or supplier self-assessment if below threshold)
Sedex SMETA audit or equivalent ethical sourcing evidence
Food waste rates, donation programmes and origin certifications (where applicable)
Sustainability commitments and targets (net-zero pathway, water, packaging)
coles.fearPoint: Coles category reviews now embed sustainability scoring — suppliers without structured data risk delisting at next review.
coles.emifyResponse: We respond to every Coles supplier sustainability request that lands in your inbox. We connect to your accounting and production systems, populate Coles' portal or questionnaire, and keep your audit-ready evidence on file for any verification.

faqs.question: What is the Coles Together to Zero supplier programme?
faqs.answer: Together to Zero is Coles' net-zero plan — covering carbon (Zero Emissions), packaging (Zero Waste) and food waste (Zero Hunger). Suppliers contribute by providing product-level emissions, packaging composition and waste data through Coles' supplier portal and questionnaires.

faqs.question: What data does Coles ask suppliers for?
faqs.answer: Scope 1, 2 and 3 emissions for supplied products; packaging recycled content and APCO compliance; Modern Slavery Act statement; Sedex SMETA audit or equivalent ethical sourcing evidence; sustainability targets and pathway. Specific asks vary by category.

faqs.question: How does Coles enforce sustainability at category review?
faqs.answer: Coles category reviews now embed sustainability scoring into ranging decisions. Suppliers with weak or missing structured data are scored down and at risk of delisting at the next review cycle.

faqs.question: Can Emify respond to Coles questionnaires for me?
faqs.answer: Yes — for a flat monthly fee from $500/month, we respond to every Coles sustainability request, populate the Coles supplier portal, complete Sedex SMETA submissions and keep your evidence audit-ready.

woolworths.name: Woolworths Group
woolworths.programmeName: Woolworths Group Sustainability Plan 2025 and Responsible Sourcing Programme
woolworths.portalName: Woolworths Supplier Portal (SAP Ariba) and Responsible Sourcing Programme questionnaires
woolworths.intro: Woolworths Group — covering Woolworths Supermarkets, BIG W and Metro — operates one of Australia's most detailed supplier sustainability programmes. Suppliers across food, beverage, household goods, packaging and services are routinely asked for emissions, ethical sourcing, packaging and modern slavery data.
whatTheyAsk: Cradle-to-gate carbon footprint for products supplied to Woolworths
Packaging recycled content and APCO-aligned recyclability data
Sedex SMETA audit and SAQ responses for ethical sourcing
Modern Slavery Act statement for suppliers above the threshold
Water use and stewardship for high-water-impact suppliers
Net-zero target year and credible reduction pathway
woolworths.fearPoint: Woolworths' Responsible Sourcing Programme is non-negotiable — non-response disqualifies your products from category lines.
woolworths.emifyResponse: We respond to Woolworths Responsible Sourcing requests, SAP Ariba portal items and ad-hoc category-team questionnaires. You stay focused on running your business; we keep Woolworths satisfied.

faqs.question: What is the Woolworths Responsible Sourcing Programme?
faqs.answer: Woolworths' Responsible Sourcing Programme covers ethical sourcing, human rights, environment and worker wellbeing for suppliers of Woolworths Group brands (Woolworths Supermarkets, BIG W, Metro). It requires Sedex SMETA audits and SAQs for high-risk supply chains.

faqs.question: What data does Woolworths ask suppliers for?
faqs.answer: Cradle-to-gate product carbon footprints; packaging recycled content and APCO recyclability; Sedex SMETA audits; Modern Slavery Act statements; water use for high-impact suppliers; net-zero target year and credible pathway.

faqs.question: Does Woolworths use SAP Ariba?
faqs.answer: Yes — Woolworths' supplier portal runs on SAP Ariba. Sustainability data flows through both Ariba and dedicated Responsible Sourcing Programme channels (Sedex, supplier questionnaires).

faqs.question: What happens if I don't respond to a Woolworths sustainability questionnaire?
faqs.answer: Non-response disqualifies your products from category lines. Woolworths' Responsible Sourcing Programme is non-negotiable for continued supply, particularly for private-label products and high-risk categories.

iga-metcash.name: IGA / Metcash
iga-metcash.programmeName: Metcash Sustainability Strategy and Supplier Code of Conduct
iga-metcash.portalName: Metcash Supplier Portal and Sustainability Questionnaires
iga-metcash.intro: Metcash supplies IGA, Foodland, Mitre 10, Total Tools and independent hardware and liquor retailers. The group has expanded its sustainability supplier requirements significantly, particularly around packaging, emissions and modern slavery.
whatTheyAsk: Scope 1, 2 and 3 emissions data for products supplied
Packaging recycled content, recyclability and APCO compliance
Modern Slavery Act statement and Supplier Code of Conduct attestation
Sustainability commitments and progress against them
iga-metcash.fearPoint: Metcash supplier reviews increasingly weight sustainability data — particularly for private-label suppliers to IGA and Foodland.
iga-metcash.emifyResponse: We respond to Metcash sustainability requests across IGA, Foodland and Mitre 10 supply relationships, keeping your category position safe.

faqs.question: Are Metcash sustainability requirements the same as Coles and Woolworths?
faqs.answer: Broadly similar (Scope 3 emissions, packaging recycled content, modern slavery) but less prescriptive. Metcash uses its Supplier Code of Conduct as the framework rather than a formal sustainability programme like Coles' Together to Zero.

faqs.question: Does Metcash use Sedex?
faqs.answer: Metcash uses Sedex SMETA for ethical sourcing audits in higher-risk categories (private-label food, household goods). Most other suppliers respond through Metcash's own questionnaire system.

faqs.question: What does Metcash sell beyond IGA?
faqs.answer: Metcash owns Foodland (SA-focused supermarkets), Mitre 10 and Total Tools (hardware), Cellarbrations and IGA Liquor (liquor). Sustainability questions cover all of these supply chains.

aldi.name: ALDI Australia
aldi.portalName: ALDI CR Supplier Programme and direct buyer questionnaires
aldi.intro: ALDI Australia runs one of the most rigorous supplier compliance programmes in Australian retail. ALDI suppliers face structured sustainability, ethical sourcing and packaging audits as a condition of continued supply.
whatTheyAsk: Packaging composition, recycled content and substitution roadmap
Ethical sourcing audits (BSCI, SMETA or equivalent)
Animal welfare certifications for meat, dairy, eggs and seafood
Modern Slavery Act statement and ALDI Code of Conduct attestation
aldi.fearPoint: ALDI's supplier compliance is a binary gate — non-compliance suspends supply.
aldi.emifyResponse: We manage the ALDI CR Supplier Programme submissions and follow-up audits so you stay continuously qualified.

faqs.question: What is the ALDI Corporate Responsibility Supplier Programme?
faqs.answer: ALDI's CR Supplier Programme is one of the most structured retailer supplier compliance programmes in Australia — covering carbon, packaging, ethical sourcing, animal welfare and supply chain due diligence. Audits are scheduled and rigorous.

faqs.question: Does ALDI accept Sedex SMETA?
faqs.answer: Yes — Sedex SMETA audits are accepted for ethical sourcing components. ALDI also accepts BSCI (amfori Business Social Compliance Initiative) audits for some categories.

faqs.question: How strict is ALDI on animal welfare?
faqs.answer: Very. ALDI publishes detailed animal welfare standards for meat, dairy, eggs and seafood — and requires supplier evidence (RSPCA Approved, MSC, ASC, Cage Free certifications) as a condition of supply.

bunnings.name: Bunnings
bunnings.intro: Bunnings Warehouse — Australia's largest home improvement retailer — requires suppliers across hardware, building materials, garden and consumables to provide structured ethical sourcing, modern slavery, timber legality and sustainability data.
whatTheyAsk: Sedex SMETA audit and SAQ responses
Timber legality and certification (FSC, PEFC) chain-of-custody
Product-level emissions data for major SKUs
Packaging recycled content and APCO compliance
bunnings.fearPoint: Bunnings has actively delisted suppliers who fail Sedex audits or fall short of timber legality.
bunnings.emifyResponse: We respond to Bunnings supplier sustainability and ethical sourcing requirements so you maintain ranging without diverting your team.

faqs.question: What is the Bunnings Responsible Sourcing Programme?
faqs.answer: Bunnings' Responsible Sourcing Programme covers ethical sourcing, modern slavery, timber legality and product safety. Sedex SMETA audits and SAQs are required for high-risk supplier categories.

faqs.question: How strict is Bunnings on timber legality?
faqs.answer: Strict — FSC and PEFC chain-of-custody evidence is required for timber and wood-product suppliers. Bunnings has actively delisted suppliers failing timber legality verification.

faqs.question: Does Bunnings require Sedex audits for every supplier?
faqs.answer: Sedex requirements depend on category risk. Building materials, garden, plumbing and hardware suppliers are typically required; lower-risk categories may use SAQ only. We coordinate per-category.

bhp.name: BHP
bhp.programmeName: BHP Local Buying Programme and Supplier Sustainability Requirements
bhp.portalName: BHP Supplier Portal (Ariba) and Local Buying Foundation
bhp.intro: BHP — operating major iron ore, coal and copper assets in WA, QLD and SA — has one of Australia's most demanding supplier sustainability programmes. Equipment suppliers, contractors, logistics providers and professional services suppliers face routine emissions, modern slavery and safety audits.
whatTheyAsk: Scope 1, 2 and 3 emissions inventory updated annually
Modern Slavery Act statement and BHP Minimum Requirements attestation
Diversity reporting including Indigenous participation in the Pilbara
Safety incident history and BHP Charter compliance
EV transition plan for fleet-based suppliers
bhp.fearPoint: BHP prequalification is non-trivial and ongoing — slipping on sustainability metrics drops you off the panel.
bhp.emifyResponse: We respond to BHP supplier sustainability requirements across Pilbara, Mount Arthur and Olympic Dam operations — maintaining your prequalification status.

faqs.question: What does BHP ask its suppliers for?
faqs.answer: Scope 1, 2 and 3 emissions inventory updated annually; Modern Slavery Act statement and BHP Minimum Requirements attestation; diversity reporting including Indigenous participation in Pilbara operations; safety incident history; EV transition plans for fleet-based suppliers; subcontractor governance.

faqs.question: What is the BHP Local Buying Programme?
faqs.answer: BHP's Local Buying Programme connects local Australian businesses to BHP's procurement — including in the Pilbara, Hunter Valley, Bowen Basin and Olympic Dam regions. Suppliers in the programme face structured sustainability and Indigenous participation requirements.

faqs.question: How does BHP enforce supplier sustainability requirements?
faqs.answer: BHP prequalification reviews are non-trivial and ongoing. Suppliers failing to maintain sustainability evidence can be dropped from the panel; slipping on Indigenous procurement participation similarly affects standing.

faqs.question: Does Emify handle the BHP Ariba supplier portal?
faqs.answer: Yes — we maintain a master sustainability data set and respond to BHP supplier sustainability requests through Ariba, the Local Buying Foundation channels and direct procurement questionnaires.

rio-tinto.name: Rio Tinto
rio-tinto.programmeName: Rio Tinto Suppliers Code of Conduct and Sustainability Reporting Standards
rio-tinto.portalName: Rio Tinto Supplier Portal (Ariba) and Sustainability Data Requests
rio-tinto.intro: Rio Tinto — operating iron ore (Pilbara), aluminium (Gladstone QAL, Yarwun) and minerals operations across Australia — embeds sustainability requirements into supplier prequalification, contract award and ongoing oversight.
whatTheyAsk: Modern Slavery Act statement and Suppliers Code of Conduct attestation
Indigenous participation plan, particularly for Pilbara and Gladstone operations
Safety performance and Rio Tinto Charter alignment
EV transition plan and low-emissions vehicle commitments
rio-tinto.fearPoint: Rio Tinto's contractor oversight has tightened materially since 2020 — sustainability and ethics gaps drive de-panelling.
rio-tinto.emifyResponse: We respond to Rio Tinto supplier requirements across Pilbara iron ore, Gladstone alumina and Bell Bay aluminium operations.

faqs.question: What does Rio Tinto ask suppliers for?
faqs.answer: Scope 1, 2 and 3 emissions inventory; Modern Slavery Act statement and Suppliers Code of Conduct attestation; Indigenous participation plans (particularly for Pilbara and Gladstone operations); safety performance; EV transition plan; water stewardship for water-intensive suppliers.

faqs.question: How has Rio Tinto's contractor oversight changed?
faqs.answer: Materially tightened since the Juukan Gorge events in 2020. Cultural heritage, Indigenous engagement and modern slavery oversight is now significantly more rigorous; supplier failures lead to de-panelling.

faqs.question: Where does Rio Tinto operate in Australia?
faqs.answer: Iron ore (Pilbara — Tom Price, Paraburdoo, Yandi, Mount Tom Price and others), aluminium (Bell Bay TAS, Boyne QLD, Tomago NSW), alumina (Yarwun QLD), and bauxite (Weipa QLD). Each site has supplier requirements tailored to its operating context.

fortescue.name: Fortescue
fortescue.programmeName: Fortescue Real Zero Strategy and Supplier Sustainability Requirements
fortescue.portalName: Fortescue Supplier Portal (Ariba) and direct sustainability data requests
fortescue.intro: Fortescue's Real Zero strategy — committing to eliminate fossil fuel use from operations by 2030 — has the most aggressive supplier decarbonisation requirements of any Australian miner. Suppliers across equipment, fuel, services and logistics are being asked to commit to or model fossil-free transitions.
whatTheyAsk: Scope 1, 2 and 3 emissions and Real Zero pathway commitment
Equipment electrification roadmap for capital suppliers
Green hydrogen supply readiness for fuel-related suppliers
Modern Slavery Act statement and Code of Conduct attestation
fortescue.fearPoint: Fortescue actively prioritises suppliers who can credibly support Real Zero — those who can't lose work to those who can.
fortescue.emifyResponse: We respond to Fortescue supplier requirements including Real Zero pathway data, so you stay credible and on-panel as the strategy ratchets.

faqs.question: What is Fortescue's Real Zero strategy?
faqs.answer: Real Zero is Fortescue's commitment to eliminate fossil fuel use from Scope 1 and 2 operations by 2030 — without offsets. It's the most aggressive net-zero commitment of any major Australian miner and has direct implications for fuel, equipment and services suppliers.

faqs.question: Does Fortescue prioritise suppliers who support Real Zero?
faqs.answer: Yes — Fortescue actively prefers suppliers who can credibly support Real Zero (electric equipment, hydrogen-ready services, decarbonising logistics). Suppliers who can't show a pathway lose work to those who can.

faqs.question: What about Fortescue Future Industries (FFI) suppliers?
faqs.answer: FFI (now operating under Fortescue Energy) is the green hydrogen, green ammonia and clean energy arm. Its suppliers face additional renewable-aligned procurement criteria.

glencore.name: Glencore Australia
glencore.programmeName: Glencore Code of Conduct and Supplier Sustainability Standards
glencore.portalName: Glencore Supplier Portal and Sustainability Data Requests
glencore.intro: Glencore operates coal (Hunter Valley, Bowen Basin), copper (Mount Isa) and zinc operations across Australia. Suppliers face annual sustainability data requests covering emissions, modern slavery and Indigenous engagement.
whatTheyAsk: Modern Slavery Act statement and Code of Conduct attestation
glencore.fearPoint: Glencore's supplier oversight has increased materially with the just-transition focus for thermal coal.
glencore.emifyResponse: We respond to Glencore supplier sustainability requirements across Hunter Valley, Mount Isa and Bowen Basin operations.

faqs.question: Where does Glencore operate in Australia?
faqs.answer: Coal (Hunter Valley NSW, Bowen Basin QLD), copper-zinc (Mount Isa QLD), zinc-lead (McArthur River NT), nickel (Murrin Murrin WA). Each operation has supplier requirements tailored to its context — Hunter Valley contractors face different requirements to Mount Isa.

faqs.question: What is Glencore's position on thermal coal supply chain ethics?
faqs.answer: Glencore has committed to a managed decline of thermal coal production, with associated just-transition supplier engagement requirements. Sustainability oversight has tightened materially since 2021.

faqs.question: What's in Glencore's Code of Conduct for suppliers?
faqs.answer: Modern slavery, anti-bribery, anti-corruption, environment, safety, human rights and Indigenous engagement. Annual attestation is required from major suppliers.

lendlease.name: Lendlease
lendlease.programmeName: Lendlease Mission Zero (net zero scope 1 & 2 by 2025, absolute zero by 2040)
lendlease.portalName: Procore, Aconex and Lendlease Subcontractor Portal
lendlease.intro: Lendlease's Mission Zero is one of the most ambitious construction sector decarbonisation programmes globally. Every subcontractor on a Lendlease project — from concrete and steel to electrical, hydraulic, fitout and façade — provides per-project carbon, modern slavery and Indigenous procurement data.
whatTheyAsk: EPDs (Environmental Product Declarations) for all major materials
Embodied carbon per unit of work supplied
ISC and Green Star rating data inputs
lendlease.fearPoint: Lendlease subcontractor selection now heavily weights carbon credentials — generic subbies lose to those who can document.
lendlease.emifyResponse: We respond to Lendlease subcontractor sustainability requirements project-by-project, keeping your work pipeline secure.

faqs.question: What is Lendlease Mission Zero?
faqs.answer: Mission Zero is Lendlease's commitment to net zero Scope 1 and 2 emissions by 2025 and absolute zero across all scopes (including Scope 3 embodied carbon) by 2040. It's one of the most ambitious construction sector net-zero targets globally.

faqs.question: What data does Lendlease ask subcontractors for?
faqs.answer: Per-project EPDs for materials supplied; embodied carbon per unit of work; Modern Slavery Act statement; Indigenous participation plans; per-project waste diversion; ISC and Green Star rating data inputs; Mission Zero alignment commitment.

faqs.question: Will I lose work with Lendlease if I can't provide carbon data?
faqs.answer: Increasingly yes — Lendlease subcontractor selection now heavily weights carbon credentials. Generic subbies lose to those who can document EPDs and embodied carbon. Mission Zero ratchets each year, so the bar rises.

faqs.question: Does Emify integrate with Procore and Aconex for Lendlease projects?
faqs.answer: Yes — Lendlease subcontractor portals run on Procore and Aconex. We respond to project sustainability data requests through both platforms.

john-holland.name: John Holland
john-holland.programmeName: John Holland Sustainability Strategy and Subcontractor Sustainability Requirements
john-holland.portalName: John Holland Subcontractor Portal, Procore and Aconex
john-holland.intro: John Holland — a major Tier 1 infrastructure and building contractor — requires subcontractors to provide per-project ISC, embodied carbon, modern slavery and Indigenous procurement data.
whatTheyAsk: ISC rating tool inputs (Design and As-Built ratings)
Embodied carbon per unit of work
john-holland.fearPoint: John Holland subcontractor reviews routinely weight sustainability scoring — generic tenders lose.
john-holland.emifyResponse: We respond to John Holland subcontractor sustainability requirements across major infrastructure and building programmes.

faqs.question: What does John Holland ask subcontractors for?
faqs.answer: EPDs for major materials, ISC rating tool inputs (Design and As-Built), embodied carbon per unit of work, Modern Slavery Act statement, Indigenous participation, per-project waste and water data. Requirements vary by project type (infrastructure vs building).

faqs.question: Does John Holland do projects across multiple states?
faqs.answer: Yes — John Holland delivers major rail, road, building and water projects in NSW, VIC, QLD, WA and SA. Each state's compliance overlay (e.g. VIC Local Jobs First, NSW APIC) layers on top of John Holland's own requirements.

faqs.question: Is John Holland's sustainability scoring competitive?
faqs.answer: Yes — sustainability and Indigenous procurement scoring now routinely affects subcontract award decisions, particularly for infrastructure projects with ISC rating targets.

multiplex.name: Multiplex
multiplex.programmeName: Multiplex Sustainability Strategy and Net Zero Roadmap
multiplex.portalName: Multiplex Subcontractor Portal, Procore and Aconex
multiplex.intro: Multiplex — Tier 1 commercial and residential builder — embeds sustainability data requirements into subcontractor prequalification and per-project reporting, including for Green Star and NABERS-rated developments.
whatTheyAsk: EPDs for materials supplied to projects
multiplex.fearPoint: Multiplex pre-qualification and tender selection actively reward sustainability data quality.
multiplex.emifyResponse: We respond to Multiplex subcontractor sustainability data requests project-by-project.

faqs.question: What sustainability data does Multiplex want from subbies?
faqs.answer: EPDs for materials, embodied carbon per work package, Modern Slavery Act statement, Indigenous participation, per-project waste data, Green Star rating inputs. Specific requirements scale with project ambition (e.g. 6-Star Green Star projects have higher data demands).

faqs.question: Does Multiplex work on government infrastructure as well as commercial buildings?
faqs.answer: Yes — Multiplex delivers commercial, residential, sports stadia, education and government building projects. Sustainability requirements typically follow Green Star and NABERS frameworks rather than the ISC framework used in road and rail infrastructure.

faqs.question: How is sustainability scored at Multiplex tender?
faqs.answer: Multiplex pre-qualification and tender selection both weight sustainability data alongside price and capability. The bar is rising annually as Multiplex's net zero programme advances.

cimic.name: CIMIC Group (CPB Contractors)
cimic.programmeName: CIMIC Group Sustainability Standards (CPB, Leighton Asia, UGL, Pacific Partnerships)
cimic.portalName: CIMIC Supplier Portal and Project Management Systems
cimic.intro: CIMIC Group — including CPB Contractors, UGL, Pacific Partnerships and Leighton Asia — is one of Australia's largest contractors. Subcontractors and equipment suppliers face structured sustainability and modern slavery data requests on every major project.
whatTheyAsk: EPDs and embodied carbon for major materials
Per-project waste, water and emissions data
cimic.fearPoint: CIMIC subcontractor selection weights sustainability data alongside price and capability.
cimic.emifyResponse: We respond to CIMIC, CPB and UGL subcontractor sustainability requirements across major infrastructure and building projects.

faqs.question: What's the difference between CIMIC, CPB, UGL and Leighton Asia?
faqs.answer: CIMIC Group is the holding entity; CPB Contractors is its Australian construction arm; UGL delivers engineering services and asset management; Leighton Asia delivers projects across Asia. Sustainability requirements broadly align across the group but with operating-company specifics.

faqs.question: Does CIMIC require Modern Slavery Act statements from all subcontractors?
faqs.answer: Above the A$100m turnover threshold, yes. Below the threshold, CIMIC requires Code of Conduct attestation and ethical sourcing self-assessment.

faqs.question: How does CIMIC integrate sustainability into project management?
faqs.answer: CIMIC operates Procore, Aconex and bespoke project management systems. Sustainability data requests flow through these systems on a per-project basis, with subcontractors uploading evidence at defined project gates.

cba.name: Commonwealth Bank (CBA)
cba.programmeName: CBA Group Sustainability Strategy and Supplier Code of Conduct
cba.portalName: CBA Supplier Portal (Coupa) and Sustainability Data Requests
cba.intro: CBA — Australia's largest bank — sources from thousands of professional services, IT, real estate and facilities suppliers. The bank's sustainability strategy includes structured supplier data requirements covering emissions, modern slavery and diversity.
whatTheyAsk: Modern Slavery Act statement and Supplier Code of Conduct attestation
Diversity reporting including gender, Indigenous participation
Information security and data sovereignty for IT suppliers
cba.fearPoint: CBA panel reviews routinely weight sustainability data — without structured responses, panel reappointment is at risk.
cba.emifyResponse: We respond to CBA supplier sustainability and modern slavery requirements across IT, professional services and facilities supply.

faqs.question: What data does CBA ask suppliers for?
faqs.answer: Scope 1, 2 and 3 emissions inventory; Modern Slavery Act statement and Supplier Code of Conduct attestation; diversity reporting including gender and Indigenous participation; information security and data sovereignty for IT suppliers; net-zero target year and pathway.

faqs.question: Does CBA use Coupa?
faqs.answer: Yes — CBA's supplier portal runs on Coupa. Sustainability data flows through Coupa alongside dedicated sustainability questionnaires for major panel reviews.

faqs.question: How often does CBA review supplier sustainability?
faqs.answer: Annual panel reviews are standard, with quarterly check-ins for major suppliers. Sustainability data quality affects panel reappointment, particularly for IT and professional services.

faqs.question: I'm a small IT supplier to CBA. Do I really need this?
faqs.answer: Yes — even small panel suppliers face the sustainability data requirements as part of panel terms. Without structured responses, panel reappointment is at risk.

westpac.name: Westpac
westpac.programmeName: Westpac Sustainability Strategy and Supplier Code of Conduct
westpac.portalName: Westpac Supplier Portal and Sustainability Data Requests
westpac.intro: Westpac sources from a deep panel of professional services, IT, real estate and facilities suppliers across Australia. The group's sustainability strategy embeds structured supplier sustainability and modern slavery requirements.
westpac.fearPoint: Westpac panel reviews increasingly weight sustainability — structured data is now a tender baseline.
westpac.emifyResponse: We respond to Westpac supplier sustainability requirements across professional services, IT and facilities supply.

faqs.question: What data does Westpac ask suppliers for?
faqs.answer: Scope 1, 2 and 3 emissions inventory; Modern Slavery Act statement; diversity reporting; information security and data sovereignty for IT/data-handling suppliers; net-zero pathway. Specific asks vary by panel category.

faqs.question: Is Westpac's supplier portal the same as CBA's?
faqs.answer: No — each major bank operates its own portal. Westpac uses its own supplier portal and Coupa-style procurement systems. Each panel category has its own onboarding sustainability questionnaire.

faqs.question: How often does Westpac review supplier sustainability?
faqs.answer: Annual panel reviews are standard, with quarterly engagement for major suppliers and additional ad-hoc reviews where Westpac's own sustainability strategy ratchets.

nab.name: National Australia Bank (NAB)
nab.programmeName: NAB Sustainability Strategy and Supplier Sustainability Programme
nab.portalName: NAB Supplier Portal and Sustainability Questionnaires
nab.intro: NAB has one of Australia's most structured supplier sustainability programmes, including formal supplier sustainability questionnaires and annual reviews for major suppliers.
whatTheyAsk: Living wage and labour rights commitments
nab.fearPoint: NAB's annual supplier sustainability reviews actively rank panel suppliers — bottom-quartile suppliers are at risk.
nab.emifyResponse: We respond to NAB supplier sustainability questionnaires and annual reviews across IT, professional services and facilities.

faqs.question: Is NAB's supplier sustainability programme stricter than other banks?
faqs.answer: NAB has one of the most structured supplier sustainability programmes among Australian banks — formal annual reviews, comparative scoring across panel suppliers, and explicit reporting requirements. The bar is consistently high.

faqs.question: Does NAB ask about living wage commitments?
faqs.answer: Yes — NAB has explicit supplier engagement on living wage and labour rights as part of its responsible business strategy. Particularly relevant for outsourced services (cleaning, catering, security).

faqs.question: What happens if I'm a bottom-quartile supplier in NAB's review?
faqs.answer: NAB's annual supplier sustainability reviews actively rank panel suppliers. Bottom-quartile suppliers receive remediation requirements; persistent underperformance affects panel reappointment.

anz.name: ANZ
anz.programmeName: ANZ Sustainability Approach and Supplier Code of Practice
anz.portalName: ANZ Supplier Portal and Sustainability Data Requests
anz.intro: ANZ sources from a panel of IT, professional services, real estate and facilities suppliers. The group's sustainability approach embeds supplier sustainability and modern slavery requirements at prequalification and panel review.
whatTheyAsk: Modern Slavery Act statement and Supplier Code of Practice attestation
anz.fearPoint: ANZ panel reviews weight sustainability data alongside price and capability.
anz.emifyResponse: We respond to ANZ supplier sustainability and modern slavery data requests.

faqs.question: What's ANZ's Supplier Code of Practice?
faqs.answer: ANZ's Supplier Code of Practice covers labour rights, environment, business integrity, modern slavery and ANZ's Reconciliation Action Plan commitments. Annual attestation required from panel suppliers.

faqs.question: Does ANZ ask for diversity data from suppliers?
faqs.answer: Yes — gender pay gap, Indigenous workforce participation, disability employment and supplier diversity (e.g. Supply Nation registration for Indigenous-owned businesses) are increasingly part of supplier sustainability data requests.

faqs.question: How does ANZ handle information security for IT suppliers?
faqs.answer: Standard ANZ Supplier Risk Management framework — including ISO 27001, SOC 2 evidence, data residency requirements and ongoing third-party risk reviews. Sustainability data sharing flows through ANZ's secure portal.

department-of-defence.name: Department of Defence
department-of-defence.programmeName: Defence Industrial Capability Programme with sustainability and modern slavery requirements
department-of-defence.portalName: AusTender, Defence Industry Portal and Sovereign Industrial Capability questionnaires
department-of-defence.intro: The Department of Defence — Australia's largest procurer — embeds sustainability, modern slavery and ethical sourcing requirements across construction, IT, professional services, catering, security, maintenance and equipment supply. Sustainability data is a tender prerequisite.
whatTheyAsk: Modern Slavery Act statement (mandatory above the threshold)
Diversity, including Indigenous Procurement Policy participation
Australian Industry Capability plans for major contracts
Information security clearances for IT and data-handling suppliers
department-of-defence.fearPoint: Defence tender evaluation embeds sustainability and modern slavery — generic responses are scored down at prequalification.
department-of-defence.emifyResponse: We respond to Defence tender sustainability schedules, AusTender questionnaires and Defence Industry Portal requests.

faqs.question: What data does the Department of Defence ask suppliers for?
faqs.answer: Modern Slavery Act statement (mandatory above the threshold); Scope 1, 2 and 3 emissions data; diversity including Indigenous Procurement Policy participation; Sovereign Industrial Capability alignment; Australian Industry Capability plans for major contracts; security clearances for data-handling suppliers.

faqs.question: What is AusTender and how do I respond to sustainability questions on it?
faqs.answer: AusTender is the central Australian Government procurement portal. Sustainability schedules are embedded into the standard tender documents. Suppliers respond with structured Scope 1/2/3 data, modern slavery declarations and diversity reporting. We respond on your behalf.

faqs.question: What is the Indigenous Procurement Policy (IPP)?
faqs.answer: The IPP sets targets for federal departments to award contracts to Indigenous businesses. Non-Indigenous suppliers report on their Indigenous subcontracting and engagement. Defence has the largest IPP commitment of any federal portfolio.

faqs.question: What is Sovereign Industrial Capability?
faqs.answer: Defence's SIC framework identifies industrial capabilities essential to defence operations. Suppliers in SIC categories face additional reporting on Australian ownership, supply chain and capability.

services-australia.name: Services Australia
services-australia.programmeName: Services Australia Procurement Framework with sustainability and modern slavery requirements
services-australia.portalName: AusTender and Services Australia Supplier Portals
services-australia.intro: Services Australia — operating Centrelink, Medicare and Child Support services — is a major procurer of IT, professional services, contact centre and facilities suppliers. Sustainability and modern slavery data are tender prerequisites.
whatTheyAsk: Diversity reporting including Indigenous Procurement Policy
Information security and ISM compliance for IT suppliers
services-australia.fearPoint: Services Australia panel reviews weight sustainability scoring — structured data is now table stakes.
services-australia.emifyResponse: We respond to Services Australia tender and panel sustainability requirements.

faqs.question: What categories does Services Australia procure?
faqs.answer: Major categories: IT services and software (including Centrelink/Medicare core systems), contact centre operations, professional services, facilities and cleaning services across hundreds of service centres nationally.

faqs.question: What is the Information Security Manual (ISM) and why does it matter for suppliers?
faqs.answer: The ISM is the Australian Government's information security framework. IT suppliers to Services Australia must demonstrate ISM compliance — sustainability data sharing also needs to respect this framework.

faqs.question: Does Services Australia weight sustainability in panel reviews?
faqs.answer: Yes — sustainability and modern slavery scoring is embedded in Services Australia's panel evaluation criteria. Structured supplier data is table stakes for major panel reappointment.

nsw-health.name: NSW Health
nsw-health.programmeName: NSW Health Net Zero Strategy and HealthShare NSW Procurement Sustainability
nsw-health.portalName: HealthShare NSW Supplier Portal and eTendering NSW
nsw-health.intro: NSW Health — encompassing local health districts, HealthShare NSW and major hospitals — procures from suppliers across medical consumables, catering, cleaning, linen, IT, professional services and construction. The NSW Health Net Zero Strategy embeds emissions and ethical sourcing requirements across the supply chain.
whatTheyAsk: Scope 1, 2 and 3 emissions data for products and services supplied
Ethical sourcing for medical consumables and uniforms
Waste handling chain-of-custody for clinical-waste-adjacent suppliers
Net-zero pathway and product-level emissions reduction commitments
nsw-health.fearPoint: HealthShare NSW tender evaluation weights sustainability — non-responses fall out of contention at prequalification.
nsw-health.emifyResponse: We respond to NSW Health and HealthShare NSW supplier sustainability requirements across catering, cleaning, linen, IT and clinical-consumable supply.

faqs.question: What is the NSW Health Net Zero Strategy?
faqs.answer: NSW Health's Net Zero Strategy commits the system to net zero emissions across owned operations and increasingly cascades supplier emissions requirements through HealthShare NSW procurement. Local Health Districts and major hospitals contribute to delivery.

faqs.question: What is HealthShare NSW?
faqs.answer: HealthShare NSW is the shared services and procurement entity for the NSW Health system. It runs supplier prequalification, manages major category contracts and embeds sustainability and modern slavery requirements into tender evaluation.

faqs.question: What sustainability data does NSW Health ask suppliers for?
faqs.answer: Scope 1, 2 and 3 emissions for products and services supplied; Modern Slavery Act statement; ethical sourcing for medical consumables and uniforms; clinical-waste chain-of-custody; diversity reporting; net-zero pathway and product-level emissions reduction commitments.

faqs.question: Is HealthShare NSW tender selection competitive on sustainability?
faqs.answer: Yes — sustainability and modern slavery scoring is now weighted in HealthShare NSW tender evaluation, alongside price, capability and risk. Suppliers without structured responses fall out of contention at prequalification.

queensland-health.name: Queensland Health
queensland-health.programmeName: Queensland Health Sustainable Healthcare Programme and procurement sustainability requirements
queensland-health.portalName: Queensland Health Supplier Portal and QTenders
queensland-health.intro: Queensland Health — covering hospitals and health services across the state — embeds sustainability and ethical sourcing requirements into supplier prequalification and contract award.
whatTheyAsk: Ethical sourcing for medical and clinical consumables
queensland-health.fearPoint: Queensland Health tender prequalification rejects suppliers without basic sustainability and modern slavery responses.
queensland-health.emifyResponse: We respond to Queensland Health and QTenders sustainability requirements.

faqs.question: What is the Queensland Health Sustainable Healthcare Programme?
faqs.answer: Queensland Health's Sustainable Healthcare Programme drives system-wide decarbonisation, waste reduction and ethical sourcing across Queensland's public hospital and health services system. Suppliers across all categories face structured sustainability data requirements.

faqs.question: What is QTenders?
faqs.answer: QTenders is the Queensland Government's central procurement portal. Sustainability and Modern Slavery requirements are embedded in standard tender documents. Suppliers respond to category-specific sustainability schedules.

faqs.question: Does Queensland Health work the same as NSW Health?
faqs.answer: Same broad framework (Scope 1/2/3, modern slavery, Indigenous participation, ethical sourcing) but different portals and slightly different prescriptive requirements. Suppliers operating in both states maintain parallel response packs.

mirvac.name: Mirvac
mirvac.programmeName: Mirvac Planet Positive (net positive carbon by 2030)
mirvac.portalName: Mirvac Supplier Portal and Sustainability Data Requests
mirvac.intro: Mirvac's Planet Positive ambition — net positive carbon by 2030 — requires every facilities, cleaning, maintenance, waste and construction supplier to provide structured emissions and ethical sourcing data.
whatTheyAsk: Embodied carbon and EPDs for construction suppliers
mirvac.fearPoint: Mirvac tender selection actively prioritises suppliers who support Planet Positive — generic providers lose work.
mirvac.emifyResponse: We respond to Mirvac supplier sustainability requirements across cleaning, maintenance, waste, security and construction supply.

faqs.question: What is Mirvac Planet Positive?
faqs.answer: Planet Positive is Mirvac's commitment to net positive carbon — meaning the group will sequester more carbon than it emits — across operations by 2030. It's one of the most ambitious REIT carbon commitments globally and cascades through to every supplier.

faqs.question: Does Mirvac use NABERS?
faqs.answer: Yes — Mirvac runs one of Australia's most extensive NABERS-rated portfolios across office, residential, retail and industrial. Suppliers provide data inputs to support NABERS rating maintenance and improvement.

faqs.question: How does Mirvac decide which suppliers support Planet Positive?
faqs.answer: Mirvac actively prioritises suppliers who can demonstrate carbon credentials, circular economy practices and Indigenous procurement. Generic providers lose work to those who can document.

dexus.name: Dexus
dexus.programmeName: Dexus Net Zero by 2030 strategy and Supplier Sustainability Standards
dexus.portalName: Dexus Supplier Portal and Sustainability Data Requests
dexus.intro: Dexus — major office, industrial and healthcare REIT — embeds sustainability and modern slavery requirements into supplier prequalification and contract award across cleaning, maintenance, waste, security and construction supply.
dexus.fearPoint: Dexus tender selection weights sustainability — suppliers without structured data are screened out.
dexus.emifyResponse: We respond to Dexus supplier sustainability requirements across cleaning, maintenance, waste and construction supply.

faqs.question: What is Dexus's net-zero commitment?
faqs.answer: Dexus has committed to net zero across owned operations by 2030. Supplier data — particularly from cleaning, maintenance, waste and construction supply — feeds into Dexus's Scope 3 reporting and Net Zero pathway.

faqs.question: Does Dexus participate in GRESB?
faqs.answer: Yes — Dexus is a long-standing GRESB participant and consistently scores in the top tier among Australian REITs. GRESB submissions need extensive supplier evidence on operational performance.

faqs.question: What office buildings does Dexus operate?
faqs.answer: Major Australian CBD and metropolitan office portfolios across Sydney, Melbourne, Brisbane, Perth and Adelaide — plus industrial and healthcare property. Suppliers across all categories face sustainability requirements.

transport-for-nsw.name: Transport for NSW
transport-for-nsw.programmeName: Transport for NSW Sustainable Procurement Framework and ISC submission requirements
transport-for-nsw.portalName: eTendering NSW and Transport for NSW Supplier Portal
transport-for-nsw.intro: Transport for NSW — Australia's largest state government transport agency — embeds ISC (Infrastructure Sustainability Council) ratings, embodied carbon, modern slavery and Indigenous procurement requirements into every major infrastructure tender and contract.
whatTheyAsk: ISC rating tool inputs (Design, As Built, Operational)
EPDs and embodied carbon for materials supplied to projects
Per-project waste, water and emissions data
transport-for-nsw.fearPoint: Transport for NSW infrastructure tenders are won and lost on sustainability scoring as much as price.
transport-for-nsw.emifyResponse: We respond to Transport for NSW supplier sustainability requirements and ISC submission inputs across major infrastructure programmes.

faqs.question: What is the Sustainable Procurement Framework at Transport for NSW?
faqs.answer: TfNSW's Sustainable Procurement Framework integrates sustainability outcomes (carbon, modern slavery, Indigenous procurement, Aboriginal Participation in Construction) into procurement evaluation across all major contracts.

faqs.question: What is APIC (Aboriginal Participation in Construction)?
faqs.answer: APIC is the NSW Government's framework requiring Aboriginal participation on major infrastructure projects — typically 1.5%+ of contract value via Aboriginal businesses and workforce. TfNSW projects routinely include APIC targets.

faqs.question: What ISC rating tools apply to TfNSW projects?
faqs.answer: ISC IS v2.1 (the current Infrastructure Sustainability rating tool) applies across Design, As Built and Operational phases. Most major TfNSW projects target an Excellent or Leading rating.

major-road-projects-victoria.name: Major Road Projects Victoria
major-road-projects-victoria.programmeName: Victoria's Big Build sustainability and Local Jobs First requirements
major-road-projects-victoria.portalName: Victorian Government Tenders and Big Build supplier portals
major-road-projects-victoria.intro: Major Road Projects Victoria — delivering the state's largest road infrastructure programmes — embeds sustainability, Local Jobs First, modern slavery and Aboriginal procurement requirements into every contract.
whatTheyAsk: EPDs and embodied carbon for materials
major-road-projects-victoria.fearPoint: Victorian Big Build tenders are scored on sustainability and social procurement alongside price.
major-road-projects-victoria.emifyResponse: We respond to Major Road Projects Victoria and Big Build sustainability requirements.

faqs.question: What is Victoria's Big Build?
faqs.answer: Big Build is the Victorian Government's umbrella for its major transport infrastructure programme — Metro Tunnel, Suburban Rail Loop, Level Crossing Removal, North East Link, Westgate Tunnel and more. Sustainability requirements apply across all projects.

faqs.question: What is Local Jobs First and how does it affect suppliers?
faqs.answer: Local Jobs First requires major Victorian Government contracts to commit to local content (locally-made materials, Victorian workers, local apprentices). Suppliers report against Local Jobs First targets as part of project sustainability reporting.

faqs.question: Does Victoria use ISC ratings?
faqs.answer: Yes — most major MRPV and Big Build projects target ISC ratings (typically Excellent). Subcontractors and material suppliers contribute to the ISC submission via per-project data inputs.
